title: Veo 2025 Performance Review -- Ridership Growth, Investment, and Equity Programs
summary: Chair Nave opens Public Transportation Committee to discuss Veo contract renewal. Veo representatives Jeffrey Hoover, Paul Kalabufo, and Reed Neil present 2025 results: $250K total new investment including $50K city revenue share (15c/trip), $130K in opportunity zone rider rebates, expanded operations staffing, and community grants to Creek Rats. Key growth metrics: 34% ridership increase, 1.7 rides/vehicle/day (nearly double 'good' benchmark), 30K+ unique riders (20% increase), median trip cost down 2%. Veo Access (low-income) memberships up 90%. Tipover detection launched March 2025 with proactive cure time of 4.5 hours (70% improvement). Virtual parking coach prevented 10K bad parking instances since August. Data shows majority of riders are NOT Syracuse University students -- ridership peaks in summer when university is out of session. Significant proportion lack driver's licenses and live in low-income households. Syracuse outperforms peer cities (Rochester, Toledo, Hartford, Cleveland) on Access program.
